description: two-sided gate corpus for the strict RFC 4648 SS4 base64 codec shipped in R50 at src/crypto/base64/base64.c (determ_base64_decode / determ_base64_encode): PASS b64 strings are mechanically generated from the raw bytes via python base64.b64encode (decoded_hex = those bytes); FAIL vectors pin one strictness class each - non-alphabet char, base64url chars, embedded whitespace/newline, missing padding, excess padding, '=' mid-string, NON-CANONICAL TRAILING BITS (the module rejects nonzero dirty bits in the final padded quantum, both pad=1 and pad=2 shapes - stricter than python binascii, which accepts QR==/QUF=), and length == 1 mod 4
source: strict RFC 4648 s4 base64 decode corpus pinning determ_base64_decode (include/determ/crypto/base64/base64.h): PASS vectors mechanically generated via base64.b64encode; FAIL vectors are the strict-reject classes (non-alphabet, base64url chars, embedded whitespace/newline, missing/excess padding, mid-string '=', non-canonical trailing bits, len % 4). Oracle: gen_base64_strict.py 2026-07-03: from-scratch strict python decoder + binascii.a2b_base64 cross-check + compiled src/crypto/base64/base64.c harness (mingw gcc 13.2.0) - all three agree on all 18 vectors
oracle: gen_base64_strict.py 2026-07-03: from-scratch strict python decoder + binascii.a2b_base64 cross-check + compiled src/crypto/base64/base64.c harness (mingw gcc 13.2.0) - all three agree on all 18 vectors

_meta.oracle: gen_base64_strict.py (generating session scratchpad): from-scratch strict python decoder (alphabet table, length%4, padding placement, dirty-bit check) cross-checked against binascii.a2b_base64 on every PASS, PLUS the compiled C module itself (mingw gcc 13.2.0 harness over src/crypto/base64/base64.c) run over all 18 vectors - all three agree
_meta.oracle_note: every PASS decodes identically under strict-python, binascii and the C module; every FAIL is rejected by strict-python (reason recorded in note) and returns -1 from determ_base64_decode; binascii ACCEPTS the two non-canonical vectors (QR== -> 41, QUF= -> 4141) - the C module is strictly stricter there, which is the contract this corpus pins
_meta.results: PASS = determ_base64_decode must return the byte count and bytes of decoded_hex (empty string -> 0 bytes); FAIL = determ_base64_decode must return -1 (reject)

vectors.note: empty string decodes to zero bytes
value whose encoding exercises '+' and '/' alphabet chars
base64url chars '-' and '_' rejected in standard mode
excess padding: three '=' in one quantum
non-canonical trailing bits, pad=2: 'QR==' has nonzero low 4 bits of 'R' ('QQ==' is the canonical encoding of 0x41)
non-canonical trailing bits, pad=1: 'QUF=' has nonzero low 2 bits of 'F' ('QUE=' is the canonical encoding of 0x4141)
